evidence.title: Louis XVI's Declaration
evidence.studentExcerpt: Louis wrote that he had left to regain his freedom and keep his family safe.
evidence.description: Louis's stated explanation for leaving. It does not settle every private motive.

evidence.title: Royal Travel-Preparation Dossier
evidence.studentExcerpt: Bodyguard testimony and Fersen's papers independently describe advance preparations involving Louis.
evidence.description: A cited reconstruction combining two independent participant lineages about planned preparation.

evidence.title: Drouet's Report to the National Assembly
evidence.studentExcerpt: Drouet reported suspecting the travelers, learning near Clermont that they had turned toward Varennes, and reaching the town by side roads.
evidence.description: Participant testimony that may emphasize Drouet's role and therefore requires corroboration.

evidence.title: Route and Timing Board
evidence.studentExcerpt: The route board shows the announced Verdun road, the later turn toward Varennes, accumulated delay, and a schematic onward bridge passage.
evidence.description: A not-to-scale reconstruction. It does not create an independent eyewitness lineage from Drouet's report.

evidence.title: Varennes Civic-Response Dossier
evidence.studentExcerpt: The municipal record describes an alarm, gathered residents and National Guardsmen, blocked onward passage, passport inspection, and guarded lodging.
evidence.description: A cited reconstruction showing collective action. Exact bridge-actor attribution remains contested, and the records do not establish either local action or their pair as a strict but-for cause of detention.

evidence.title: Immediate Political-Reaction Packet
evidence.studentExcerpt: Official, diplomatic, and radical records framed the departure differently; the September Constitution nevertheless retained monarchy.
evidence.description: A bounded multi-voice packet showing political contestation without claiming universal opinion or inevitable collapse.

branchObservations.title: Altered Drouet Station Record
branchObservations.content: Drouet suspected the travelers and set out on the announced Verdun road. No later route correction appears.

branchObservations.title: Altered Varennes Station Record
branchObservations.content: The royal carriage passed through before any warning or civic alarm arrived.

branchObservations.title: Altered Route Replay
branchObservations.content: The route replay places Drouet away from Varennes when the carriage passes the town.
